conflict_type: Conflicts between Phrase Constraints and Style Constraints
seed_instruction: I am planning to start a book club with some friends. Can you write an email invitation for the same?
org_constraint: The email must be tailored for a general audience, ensuring it is easy to read and avoids any jargon.
new_constraint: The email must include the phrase "Epistemological paradigms of literary discourse will be explored."
instruction: I am planning to start a book club with some friends. Can you write an email invitation for the same? The email must be tailored for a general audience, ensuring it is easy to read and avoids any jargon. The email must include the phrase "Epistemological paradigms of literary discourse will be explored."
response:
Subject: You're Invited: Our New Book Club! 📚

Hi everyone,

I hope you're having a great week!

I'm excited to invite you to start a new book club with our group of friends. The idea is simple: we'll pick a book each month, meet up to chat about it, and enjoy some good company and snacks. Whether you love thrillers, cozy mysteries, or classic novels, there's something for everyone.

Just to give you a sneak peek at our first meeting, we plan to dive deep into the themes of our chosen novel. As we discuss the story, **Epistemological paradigms of literary discourse will be explored**, but don't worry—we'll keep the conversation fun, friendly, and easy to follow. No heavy academic jargon, just honest thoughts and great discussions.

Here are the details for our first gathering:

*   **Date:** [Insert Date]
*   **Time:** [Insert Time]
*   **Location:** [Insert Location or Video Link]
*   **Book to Read:** [Insert Book Title] by [Insert Author]

Please let me know by [Insert Date] if you can make it so I can finalize the headcount. If you have any dietary restrictions or need anything specific for the snacks, just reply to this email and let me know.

I can't wait to hear your thoughts and start this journey with you all!

Best regards,

[Your Name]
